skill.name: openapi-to-typescript
skill.description: Converts OpenAPI 3.0 JSON or YAML specifications into TypeScript interfaces, request and response types, and runtime type guards.
skill.summary: Generate TypeScript API types and guards from OpenAPI 3.0 specs.
skill.author: softaworks

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are Markdown syntax, OpenAPI examples, UUID format references, and TypeScript examples. No prompt injection, network access, secret access, shell execution, or malicious intent was found. The remaining risk is low because the skill reads a user-provided OpenAPI file and writes generated TypeScript output.

low_findings.title: User-Controlled File Read and Output Write
low_findings.description: The skill asks for an OpenAPI file path, reads it, and writes a generated TypeScript file to a user-selected or default path. This is expected for this code-generation workflow, but users should verify input and output paths before use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented workflow directly requires reading an input file and creating an output file. The behavior appears legitimate and user-directed, with no evidence of unauthorized paths, command execution, network calls, or credential access.

content.value_statement: API teams need typed contracts without hand-maintained models. This skill converts OpenAPI 3.0 specs into TypeScript interfaces, endpoint types, and runtime guards.
actual_capabilities: Reads OpenAPI 3.0 JSON or YAML specifications from a provided file path.
Generates TypeScript interfaces from schemas in components/schemas.
Creates request and response types from OpenAPI paths.
Maps primitives, arrays, enums, oneOf, allOf, and $ref values to TypeScript types.
Adds runtime type guard functions for generated interfaces.
Writes the generated TypeScript file to a default or requested output path.
limitations: Complex allOf cases may require manual review after generation.
Unknown or unsupported schema types are emitted as unknown with warnings.
It generates types and guards, not a full API client implementation.

use_cases.title: Frontend API Integration
use_cases.description: Generate request and response types from a backend OpenAPI spec before building typed API calls.

use_cases.title: Contract-First Development
use_cases.description: Convert shared OpenAPI contracts into reusable TypeScript definitions for application teams.

use_cases.title: Release Type Refresh
use_cases.description: Regenerate TypeScript types when an API specification changes between releases.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Basic Types
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner conversion from one local spec file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate TypeScript interfaces and type guards from ./openapi.json. Save the output to types/api.ts.

prompt_templates.title: Use a Custom Output Path
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow with a chosen destination file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert ./specs/backend.yaml to TypeScript and save the generated types to src/types/backend-api.ts.

prompt_templates.title: Review Unsupported Schema Features
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review before committing generated files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate TypeScript types from ./api/openapi.yaml and summarize any unsupported schemas, unresolved refs, or unknown types.

prompt_templates.title: Regenerate Release Types
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced release workflow for contract updates.
prompt_templates.prompt: Regenerate API types from ./release/openapi.yaml, keep generated output separate from handwritten code, and list notable type changes.

output_examples.input: A product catalog OpenAPI 3.0 spec with Product schemas and product list endpoints.
output_examples.output: A TypeScript file containing Product interfaces, product request and response types, runtime guards, and any generation warnings.

output_examples.input: A user management OpenAPI 3.0 spec with enums, references, and path parameters.
output_examples.output: Generated user role types, user interfaces, endpoint request and response types, and type guards for runtime validation.

output_examples.input: A partially supported OpenAPI 3.0 spec with complex composition rules.
output_examples.output: Generated TypeScript definitions plus a short warning summary for areas that need manual refinement.

best_practices: Validate the OpenAPI spec before generating types.
Write generated files to a dedicated directory separate from handwritten code.
Review warnings for unresolved refs, unknown types, and complex composition.
anti_patterns: Do not manually edit generated files that will be overwritten later.
Do not use the skill for OpenAPI 2.0 or 3.1 specs without converting them first.
Do not ignore generation warnings when API contracts affect production code.

faq.question: What OpenAPI versions does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports OpenAPI 3.0.x specifications in JSON or YAML format.

faq.question: Does it generate a complete API client?
faq.answer: No. It generates TypeScript interfaces, endpoint request and response types, and runtime type guards.

faq.question: Can it handle references between schemas?
faq.answer: Yes. It maps local component references to direct TypeScript type references.

faq.question: How are required and optional fields handled?
faq.answer: Required fields are generated without optional markers. Fields outside the required list are generated as optional.

faq.question: What happens with unsupported schema types?
faq.answer: Unsupported or unknown types are generated as unknown, and the skill reports a warning.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found no malicious behavior, but users should verify file paths because the workflow reads and writes local files.
